params.text:
Hazard pictograms and warning labels

Products that contain hazardous chemicals must be labelled under the CLP Regulation (Classification, Labelling and Packaging), which uses the internationally agreed GHS pictograms. A pictogram is a red-bordered diamond with a black symbol on a white background. The label also carries signal words such as "Danger" or "Warning", plus hazard and precautionary statements.

Learning to read these symbols at a glance tells you how to treat a product before you even open it. Always check the label and follow the instructions.

params.alt: Cleaning products that can be hazardous

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: Match each CLP/GHS hazard pictogram to what it warns you about.

dialogs.answer: Can cause severe skin burns and serious eye damage, and may corrode metals. Think strong bleach, drain and oven cleaners. Wear gloves and eye protection.
Can cause serious harm or death even in small amounts if swallowed, inhaled or contacting skin. Rare in care but may appear on some pest-control products.
May cause skin or eye irritation, allergic skin reactions, drowsiness or mild toxicity. Common on many everyday cleaning products.
Catches fire easily. Includes alcohol hand gels, aerosols and some cleaning sprays. Keep away from heat, sparks and naked flames.
Can cause serious longer-term effects such as breathing problems, cancer or damage to fertility or organs. Handle strictly per the label and SDS.

dialogs.text: Hazardous to the environment (dead tree and fish)
dialogs.answer: Harmful to aquatic life and the wider environment. Never pour these substances down the sink or drain; dispose of them correctly.
